event: severe pain in lower abdomen with tenderness
no fever, masses or lymph nodes
whole extent of the bowel was dilated
no free liquid, air or masses
full extent of the small bowel had a purplish color and was edematous
engorged and had areas of ecchymosis in the mesentery
bowel had visible peristalsis and responded to reanimation maneuvers
planned to maintain a continuous assessment of the bowel vitality
transferred to the intensive care unit
angio-3D reconstruction of a patient’s CT scan
no thrombus or embolus was discovered
vascular malformation of the superior mesenteric artery was found
superior mesenteric artery and the celiac trunk arise from a common trunk
24 hours later, on the second laparotomy
bowel loops were in better condition and regained their normal color and mobility
despite treatment and anticoagulation, he never fully recovered from his acidosis
led to sepsis and refractory shock resulting in organ failure
